content.problemStatement:
You are the owner of a big company. You are so rich, that the government has allowed you to print as many notes as you want of any single value that you like. You also have peculiar behavioral traits and you often do things that look weird to a third person.

You have NNN employees, where the iii-th employee has salary AiA_iAi​. You want to pay them using a denomination that you create. You are also eco-friendly and wish to save paper. So, you wish to pay them using as few notes as possible. Find out the minimum number of notes required if you can alter the salary of at most one employee to any **positive integer** that you like, and choose the positive integer value that each note is worth (called its denomination).

Each employee must receive the exact value of his/her salary and no more.

Subtasks
constraints: The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, where AiA_iAi​ is the salary of the iii-th employee.
For each test case, output in a single line the answer to the problem.
The sum of NNN over all test cases is at most 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: We can change the salary of the third person to 111 and use 111 as the denomination. So in total we need 11+21+11\frac{1}{1} + \frac{2}{1} + \frac{1}{1}11​+12​+11​ = 1+2+11 + 2 + 11+2+1 = 444 notes.
Test Case 222: We can change the salary of the first person to 222 and use 222 as the denomination. So in total we need 1+2+11 + 2 + 11+2+1 = 444 notes.
Test Case 333: We can use 222 as the denomination and we need not change the salary of any person. So in total we need 1+11 + 11+1 = 222 notes.
